commit.message:
fix: deferred docs stuck as pending — extracted_at set without extraction

DocumentProcessor unconditionally set extracted_at on all docs including
deferred ones (skip_extraction=True). This made the verification hub's
OCR button filter (!d.extracted_at) exclude them, so they appeared as
pending but couldn't be processed.

Fixes:
- Don't set extracted_at for deferred extraction docs
- Widen docsNeedingExtraction filter to catch deferred/pending docs
- Backfilled 139 stuck docs (cleared bogus extracted_at)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

with the browser/OS setting. No JS required.

commit.message:
Move all player data to MySQL database

Major architecture change: all player stats (score, upgrades, lives,
godmode, totems) are now stored server-side in MySQL instead of
client localStorage. localStorage only stores the auth token.

Server changes:
- Player stats loaded from DB on login, saved on disconnect
- Periodic auto-save every 30 seconds
- Upgrades validated and applied server-side (buy_upgrade message)
- Bomb damage calculated server-side (prevents cheating)
- Godmode tracked server-side (use_godmode message)
- Reset save handled server-side (reset_save message)
- value_multiplier read from server player data, not from client

Client changes:
- Removed all localStorage game data save/load
- Receives player_data from server on login and after changes
- Shop sends buy_upgrade requests instead of applying locally
- Bomb damage applied from server response
- collect message no longer sends value_multiplier

Fixes:
- Score persists across browsers/devices
- No more coin loss on page reload
- Score preserved when logging in from different browser

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(comments): getPublishedLocations now resolves composite_id via UUID fallback

Clients pass the published composite_id as parent_id when commenting on
memories in spaces. getPublishedLocations only searched by original_memory_id
property, so it found nothing and threw 'Parent memory is not published'.

Now falls back to a direct fetchObjectById lookup when the original_memory_id
filter returns no results, supporting both original and composite IDs.

commit.message:
fix: use invalidateQueries instead of removeQueries for physics cache

removeQueries disconnects active observers, preventing automatic
refetch when equipment changes. invalidateQueries with refetchType
'all' triggers an immediate background refetch for active observers,
even with staleTime: Infinity.

Fix: bind Next.js to Railway's PORT in package.json start script

Railway injects PORT=8080 (not 3000). railway.toml startCommand is
overridden by Railway dashboard config, so the fix must live in
package.json. next start -p ${PORT:-3000} picks up Railway's PORT
env var directly.

commit.message:
Add cosmogram 61-branch architecture: spec, scaffold script v2, contract prompt, and 42 stub files (#110)

Three new artifacts:
- scl-deep/cosmogram-architecture.md — The 61-branch knowledge system spec. Defines the
  polysemy principle, deep register tables for all 61 emojis (Orders/Axes/Types/Colors/
  Blocks/Operators with Liberal Arts, column orders, seasons, hermeneutic senses,
  cognitive postures, monthly cycle), file format, branch ordering, density expectations,
  cross-cosmogram threading format, and scriptability contract.

- scripts/scaffold-cosmograms-v2.py — Generates 42 cosmogram-v2 stub files pre-populated
  from existing repo data (zip-registry.json + weight-vectors.json). Each file contains
  61 branches in fixed order with weight-derived affinity labels (home territory, strong
  affinity, moderate, neutral, thin, hard suppression), pre-computed cross-cosmogram
  threads (same-Order and same-Axis adjacencies), and core resonance stubs.
  Flags: --deck N, --all, --overwrite, --dry-run.

- seeds/cosmogram-contract-prompt-v1.md — Temp architect research session contract.
  61-branch structure matching the architecture spec. Full polysemy register tables.
  4-phase protocol: pre-search synthesis → branch research → threading → density map.
  Supersedes domain-based seeds/cosmogram-research-prompt.md for v2 format.

Also generated: 42 deck-cosmograms/deck-[01-42]-cosmogram-v2.md stub files using the new
scaffold script. All 42 decks have 61 branches seeded with affinity data from the weight
vector engine. Ready for research population via Genspark temp architect sessions.

Whiteboard updated: cosmogram architecture tasks marked DONE; first deck cosmogram
marked ACTIVE (ready to run with web access).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf(api): réduction majeure de la latence conversations et messages

conversations: remplace N requêtes séquentielles (1 par conv pour le dernier
message) par 2 requêtes parallèles. Avant: O(N) aller-retours. Après: O(1).

messages: vérification participation + fetch messages en parallèle (Promise.all),
mise à jour last_read_at en fire-and-forget pour ne plus bloquer la réponse.

commit.message:
feat: default new uploads to store audio on user's PDS (#1060)

PDS audio uploads were opt-in; flip the default so new uploads go to
the user's PDS automatically. Users who have never toggled the setting
get PDS uploads; users who explicitly disabled it stay opted out.
Falls back to R2-only if the PDS rejects the blob (too large).

decision that was never properly justified and created unnecessary
architectural friction (e.g. [WAKE-CLAUDE] indirection, blind coordinators).

- INTERCOM for local Roo <-> Claude Code communication
- RooSync for inter-machine communication

commit.message:
fix: give binance_futures/spot dedicated crons — too slow for batch group

binance_futures and binance_spot need full 300s timeout due to VPS proxy
fallback (~100s per invocation for 17 pages × 3 periods). They were timing
out within the batch's ~70s per-platform limit.

- Add dedicated crons: binance_futures at :45, binance_spot at :48 (every 3h)
- Remove from batch group A (now just bitget_futures + okx_futures)
- Group A with 2 platforms gets ~140s per platform — sufficient

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix CI/CD: narrow pkill pattern to avoid killing deploy shell

pkill -f "standalone/server.js" matched the bash process running the
deploy script (script text is in bash's cmdline argv). This caused the
shell to receive SIGTERM mid-execution, closing the SSH connection with
exit 255. Now using "node.*standalone/server.js" to only match the
actual Node.js server process.

commit.message:
feat(agent): implement Quality Monitor + Security Review agents (Phase 5+6)

Quality Agent (Phase 5):
- Weighted health scoring (signal_to_noise, tp_rate, cost, recency, stability)
- Action recommendations: HEALTHY/TUNE/REVIEW/RETIRE/INVESTIGATE
- Cross-agent journal reading for pipeline-wide insights
- Daily markdown reports at monitoring/reports/YYYY-MM-DD.md
- Auto-transitions DEPLOYED -> MONITORING for healthy detections

Security Agent (Phase 6):
- Secret scanning with configurable regex patterns
- Code security review (injection, SSL, permissions)
- Detection rule security checks (broad exclusions, process-name-only filters)
- Supply chain / dependency change flagging
- Pipeline integrity verification (cross-agent modification detection)
- PII scanning in test fixtures
- Structured PR comments with PASS/WARN/BLOCK verdicts
- Audit logging to security/audit-log.jsonl
- Configurable scan patterns via security/scan-patterns.yml
